name: TitleMax Title Pawns
description_short: TitleMax offers short-term title pawns and loans using vehicle titles as collateral, providing fast cash to borrowers with bad or no credit in 14–16 states.
description_long:
TitleMax was founded in 1998 in Columbus, Georgia, and has grown into one of the largest title lending and pawn operators in the United States. Headquartered in Savannah, Georgia, the company operates under its parent TMX Finance LLC (formerly TitleMax Holdings) and runs 900–1,100+ store locations across 14–16 states, including Alabama, Arizona, Georgia, Mississippi, Missouri, Nevada, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, and others. TitleMax holds no CDFI, HUD-approved, NFCC, or nonprofit certifications — it is a for-profit commercial lender and pawn operator subject to state pawn and lending statutes and federal CFPB oversight.

At the Marietta, Georgia location, TitleMax operates under Georgia's pawn shop statutes, meaning transactions are legally classified as pawns rather than traditional consumer loans — a distinction that significantly reduces standard borrower protections. Customers bring a vehicle with a free-and-clear title, receive cash typically ranging from $1,000 to $10,000 (up to $25,000 for qualified borrowers in select markets), and keep their vehicle while TitleMax holds the title as collateral. No credit check is required, no minimum credit score applies, and no bank account is needed to qualify. The company also offers motorcycle title loans and pawns, and provides unsecured personal loans or lines of credit in select states outside the pawn-statute markets. Funds can be disbursed in as little as 30 minutes.

TitleMax's primary differentiators are speed and accessibility. By serving applicants with bad credit, no credit, or no bank account, TitleMax reaches consumers largely excluded from traditional bank lending and credit cards. Its mobile app — developed by TMX Finance LLC with 500,000+ Android downloads — allows customers to check balances, make payments via debit or credit card, view payment history, and find store locations. The Marietta location carries a 5.0/5 Google rating from 637 reviews, reflecting strong local service interactions despite the company's broader controversy.

TitleMax is a product of last resort, not a recommended primary borrowing strategy. Interest rates run up to 25% per month, producing effective APRs commonly cited between 119% and 300%+. Georgia's pawn statute classification means borrowers operate with fewer legal protections than standard consumer lending laws would afford. In 2023, the CFPB ordered TMX Finance LLC to pay a $10 million penalty for unlawful title loan practices, including overcharging active-duty military families in violation of the Military Lending Act. Consumer review platforms show approximately 2.0/5 stars with high complaint volumes, and TitleMax is not BBB accredited at the corporate level. Investigative reporting, including ProPublica coverage, has documented how these products can trap lower-income borrowers in extended debt cycles. Borrowers who cannot repay quickly face steep compounding costs and risk losing their vehicle title.
address: 2052 S Cobb Dr SE, Marietta, GA 30060

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee applies — this is a lending and pawn product, not a subscription service.

pros: Founded in 1998 with 25+ years of title lending experience and 900–1,100+ locations nationwide
No credit check or minimum credit score required — bad credit and no credit accepted
No bank account required to qualify for a title pawn or loan
Vehicle stays in the customer's possession while the title is held as collateral
Cash disbursement in as little as 30 minutes at in-store locations
Mobile app (iOS and Android, 500,000+ Android downloads) supports payments, balance checks, and payment history
Marietta location holds a 5.0/5 Google rating from 637 reviews
cons: Interest rates up to 25% per month, with effective APRs commonly ranging from 119% to 300%+ annually
CFPB enforcement action in 2023 resulted in a $10 million penalty for unlawful lending practices and overcharging military families
Operates under Georgia pawn shop statutes at this location, providing fewer consumer protections than standard lending laws
Not BBB accredited; consumer review platforms show approximately 2.0/5 stars with high complaint volumes
Defaulting borrowers risk losing their vehicle title and ultimately their vehicle
best_for: Vehicle owners with a free-and-clear title who need fast emergency cash and have no viable lower-cost alternative
Borrowers with bad credit or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans or credit cards
Consumers without a bank account who need immediate in-store cash access
Georgia residents already familiar with pawn-statute-based title products and their legal framework
diagnosis: TitleMax is genuinely accessible to consumers with no credit, bad credit, or no bank account who own a vehicle outright and need cash quickly — the 30-minute funding and no-credit-check model fills a real gap that banks do not serve. However, with APRs frequently exceeding 200%, a 2023 CFPB enforcement action on record, and pawn-statute classification in Georgia that reduces borrower protections, this is a high-cost last-resort product that can compound rapidly for borrowers who cannot repay within the first billing cycle.
services: Car Title Pawns (Georgia pawn statute, title held as collateral)
Car Title Loans (traditional loan structure, select states)
Lines of Credit (unsecured, select states only)
In-store cash disbursement (funds in as little as 30 minutes)
Online customer account portal (balance, payment history, due dates)
Mobile app for iOS and Android (payments, balance checks, store locator)
Store-based loan servicing and account management
